value.found_quote: **`toolPermission`** | string | `"request-review"` | Global safety presets: `"request-review"` (prompts for write/bash/web tools), `"proceed-in-sandbox"` (auto-proceed inside sandbox), `"always-proceed"` (never prompts), or `"strict"` (prompts for all non-read tools).
value.rationale: The page explicitly documents a `toolPermission` configuration key described as "Global safety presets" with multiple options including `"request-review"`, `"always-proceed"`, and `"strict"`, which controls when the agent prompts for approval before using tools. Additionally, the `/permissions` slash command is documented as: "Switch between global permission presets (`request-review`, `always-proceed`, `strict`)." This directly substantiates the claim about configurable global safety presets controlling when the agent prompts for approval before using tools.
